name: Medallions of Veal with Pistachio Butter Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds Boneless veal loin, trimmed and tied at room temperature
8 ounces Cold unsalted butter, cut into 16 pieces
1/2 cup Coarsely chopped, toasted pistachio nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a heavy roasting pan. Season the veal with salt and pepper.
3. Brown the meat on all sides over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes.
4. Place the meat in the oven and roast for about 30 minutes, or until a thermometer inserted into the thickest portion registers 140 degrees Fahrenheit. Baste with butter several times during roasting.
5. Remove the meat from the oven, cover loosely with foil, and allow to rest in a warm place for 15 minutes before slicing.
6. Slice the veal into 1/4-inch thick medallions and serve 2 per person with Pistachio Butter Sauce spooned over top. Serves 8 to 10.
PISTACHIO BUTTER SAUCE
1. In a small, heavy saucepan, combine shallots, white Burgundy wine, white wine vinegar, water, salt, and pepper.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil and reduce heat. Simmer until the mixture is reduced to about 2 tablespoons.
3. Cool slightly. Over the lowest possible heat, begin whisking in the pieces of cold butter one at a time. Do not add more butter until each piece is incorporated.
4. The sauce will have the consistency of heavy cream after all the butter has been added. Whisk in the garlic and pistachios. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
5. Makes about 1 1/2 cups. Sauce may be kept lukewarm in a double boiler. At serving time, place over heat and whisk in 1 teaspoon cold water at a time until the desired consistency is achieved.
